ShortDescription: This law amends several laws to integrate environmental taxes into the  fiscal framework. It modifies the Environmental Management law and other tax and administrative laws to implement the new environmental levies on groundwater, waste, fuels, and uranium-235.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental Taxes Law; Environmental Management Law
SpecificTopic: Water Quality Standards; Waste Reduction and Recycling; Air Quality

ShortTitle: Cross-Border Air Pollution Convention Approval Law (1982)
OriginalTitle: Wet van 26 april 1982, houdende de goedkeuring van het op 13 november 1979 te Genève tot stand gekomen Verdrag betreffende grensoverschrijdende luchtverontreiniging over lange afstand
ShortDescription: This  law formally approves the Convention on Long-range Transboundary Air Pollution, which was adopted in Geneva on November 13, 1979. The approval is necessary according to the  Constitution before the Convention can be ratified. By passing this law, the Netherlands integrates an international agreement aimed at addressing and reducing air pollution that travels across national borders into its domestic legal framework, demonstrating its commitment to international environmental cooperation.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Convention on Long-range Transboundary Air Pollution (Geneva, 1979)
SpecificTopic: Air Quality; Transboundary Air Pollution Control; Emission Reduction Obligations
ApplicableSectors: Public Sector; Energy Production and Utilities; Manufacturing

OriginalTitle: Wet van 26 februari 2011, houdende regels omtrent energie-efficiëntie (Wet implementatie EG-richtlijnen energie-efficiëntie)
ShortDescription: This Act implements EU Directive 2006/32/EC on energy efficiency and energy services. It mandates individual metering, transparent billing, and sets standards for appliances. It grants the competition authority enforcement powers, including fines.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: EU Directive 2006/32/EC; Energy Saving Appliances Act
SpecificTopic: Energy Efficiency Standards; Industrial Energy Efficiency and Energy Audits; Compliance with Laws and Regulations
ApplicableSectors: Electronics; Manufacturing; Energy Production and Utilities

ShortTitle: Equal Treatment on Disability or Chronic Illness Law (2003)
OriginalTitle: Wet van 3 april 2003 tot vaststelling van de Wet gelijke behandeling op grond van handicap of chronische ziekte
ShortDescription: This law prohibits discrimination based on disability or chronic illness in employment, education, and public services. It mandates reasonable accommodations unless these cause disproportionate burden. Discriminatory contracts and terminations can be nullified.
SpecificTopic: Non-Discrimination; Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities; Fair Labor Practices

OriginalTitle: Wet van 3 juli 1986, houdende regelen inzake bescherming van de bodem (Wet bodembescherming)
ShortDescription: This  law establishes comprehensive regulations for protecting the soil from contamination and degradation. Its main goal is to prevent, limit, or remedy harmful changes in the soil’s chemical, physical, or biological properties. It sets general rules for activities that may contaminate or harm the soil, such as adding or removing substances, construction, and hazardous material transport. It also provides for soil quality standards, emergency measures for serious contamination, and designation of protected soil and groundwater zones.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Economic Offenses Law; General State Taxes Law
SpecificTopic: Contaminated Land Remediation; Soil Conservation; Soil Quality Standards
ApplicableSectors: Construction; Agriculture, Farming, and Fishing; Manufacturing

OriginalTitle: Wet van 30 november 1994 tot aanvulling van de Boeken 3, 6 en 8 van het Burgerlijk Wetboek met regels betreffende de aansprakelijkheid voor gevaarlijke stoffen en verontreiniging van lucht, water of bodem
ShortDescription: This law amends the Civil Code to introduce strict liability for damages caused by hazardous substances and pollution. It applies to professional users, landfill operators, and borehole operators. It details recoverable damages, preventive actions, and conditions for limited or joint responsibility.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Civil Code; Law on Environmentally Hazardous Substances
SpecificTopic: Hazardous Substances; Contaminated Land Remediation; Water Quality Standards
ApplicableSectors: Manufacturing; Energy Production and Utilities; Construction; Chemicals

OriginalTitle: Wet van 5 maart 1998, houdende regels ter bescherming van het Antarctisch milieu ter uitvoering van het Protocol betreffende milieubescherming bij het Verdrag inzake Antarctica (Wet bescherming Antarctica)
ShortDescription: This law establishes rules for environmental protection in Antarctica and implements the Environmental Protocol to the Antarctic Treaty. It prohibits nuclear explosions, mineral exploration, and waste disposal in Antarctica. It mandates environmental assessments, permits, incident reporting, and includes violations under economic offenses.
SpecificTopic: Marine Environment Protection; Biodiversity Conservation; Compliance with Laws and Regulations
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E4: Biodiversity and Ecosystems; ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources

ShortTitle: North Sea Oil Pollution Agreement Law (1973)
OriginalTitle: WET van 7 september 1973, houdende goedkeuring van de op 9 juni 1969 te Bonn tot stand gekomen Overeenkomst betreffende samenwerking bij het bestrijden van verontreiniging van de Noordzee door olie, met bijlage (Trb. 1969, 139).
ShortDescription: This law approves the Bonn Agreement of June 9, 1969, which concerns cooperation among signatory states in combating oil pollution of the North Sea. The agreement aims to facilitate mutual assistance and coordination of measures to prevent, control, and clean up oil pollution incidents, thereby protecting the marine environment. By approving this agreement, the Netherlands commits to its provisions, integrating international environmental protection efforts into national law.
SpecificTopic: Marine Pollution; Water Quality Standards
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E2: Pollution; ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
ApplicableSectors: Maritime Transport; Energy Production and Utilities

ShortTitle: Equal Treatment by Age in Work and Education Law (2003)
OriginalTitle: Wet van 17 december 2003, houdende gelijke behandeling op grond van leeftijd bij arbeid, beroep en beroepsonderwijs (Wet gelijke behandeling op grond van leeftijd bij de arbeid)
ShortDescription: This law implements EU Directive 2000/78/EC to prohibit age discrimination in employment, professions, and vocational education. It applies to job terms, promotions, and conditions, allowing justified exceptions. Violations make contractual terms voidable or null.
SpecificTopic: Non-Discrimination; Fair Labor Practices; Compliance with Laws and Regulations

OriginalTitle: Wet van 29 december 1982, houdende tijdelijke regelen inzake sanering in geval van verontreiniging van de bodem (Interimwet bodemsanering)
ShortDescription: This  law, known as the Interim Soil Remediation law, establishes temporary regulations for the remediation of soil pollution in the Netherlands. Its primary objective is to urgently address or prevent soil contamination and its harmful consequences, especially when it poses a serious risk to public health or the environment. The act mandates provincial authorities to create annual five-year remediation programs, identifying contaminated sites and outlining necessary measures, investigations, and estimated costs. It also includes provisions for governmental oversight, municipal involvement, and powers to issue orders for investigation and cleanup, along with financial mechanisms for cost recovery and compensation.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental Hygiene General Provisions Law (1981); Claims Law (1962)
SpecificTopic: Contaminated Land Remediation; Compliance with Laws and Regulations; Soil Quality Standards
ApplicableSectors: Construction; Agriculture, Farming, and Fishing; Manufacturing

OriginalTitle: Rijkswet van 21 maart 2002, houdende goedkeuring van het op 11 december 1997 te Kyoto totstandgekomen Protocol van Kyoto bij het Raamverdrag van de Verenigde Naties inzake klimaatverandering (Trb. 1998, 170 en 1999, 110)
ShortDescription: This law approves the Kyoto Protocol to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change, committing the Netherlands to binding targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions. It fulfills the constitutional requirement for parliamentary approval before ratifying international treaties and enables national implementation of the Protocol’s obligations.
SpecificTopic: Climate Change Mitigation; Emission Reduction Obligations; Compliance with Laws and Regulations
ApplicableSectors: Public Sector; Energy Production and Utilities; Manufacturing

ShortTitle: Oil Pollution Fund Protocol Approval Law (2005)
OriginalTitle: Rijkswet van 28 april 2005 tot goedkeuring van het op 16 mei 2003 te Londen tot stand gekomen Protocol bij het Internationaal Verdrag betreffende de instelling van een Internationaal Fonds voor vergoeding van schade door verontreiniging door olie, 1992
ShortDescription: This law approves the Protocol to the 1992 International Convention on the Establishment of an International Fund for Compensation for Oil Pollution Damage, concluded in London in 2003. It mandates States General approval as required by the Constitution and notes that adjustments to compensation limits per Article 24 of the Protocol do not require additional approval.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: 1992 Oil Pollution Compensation Fund Convention; Constitution Article 91
SpecificTopic: Marine Pollution; Hazardous Substances; Compliance with Laws and Regulations
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E2: Pollution; ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources

ShortTitle: Worst Forms of Child Labor Convention Approval Law (2000)
OriginalTitle: Rijkswet van 21 december 2000 tot goedkeuring van het op 17 juni 1999 te Genève tot stand gekomen Verdrag betreffende het verbod en de onmiddellijke actie voor de uitbanning van de ergste vormen van kinderarbeid (Verdrag nr. 182 aangenomen door de Internationale Arbeidsconferentie in haar zevenentachtigste zitting)
ShortDescription: This law approves ILO Convention No. 182 on prohibiting and eliminating the worst forms of child labour. It legally binds the Netherlands to the Convention’s provisions. Implementation occurs through separate national legislation.
SpecificTopic: Child Labor; Fair Labor Practices; Compliance with Laws and Regulations
ApplicableSectors: All Industries (Sector-Agnostic); Agriculture, Farming, and Fishing; Manufacturing

OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 4 juli 1985, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Uitvoeringsbesluit verontreiniging rijkswateren (Stb. 1970, 536) zoals dit laatstelijk is gewijzigd bij Koninklijk besluit van 7 januari 1985, Stb. 51
ShortDescription: This  decree concerns the Implementation Regulation on Pollution of State Waters, updating the 1970 decree, last amended in January 1985. It defines its scope to include surface waters managed by the state, territorial waters, and parts of the high seas. It establishes procedures for obtaining permits for the discharge of waste or pollutants, specifying the required application data. It also creates a levy for state water pollution based on pollution value calculated using chemical and biological oxygen demand and nitrogen equivalents, with rates applied to various industries.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Surface Water Pollution Law 1969; General State Taxes Law 1959
SpecificTopic: Water Quality Standards; Marine Pollution; Wastewater Treatment
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E2: Pollution; ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources

OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 8 januari 2008, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Uitvoeringsbesluit belastingen op milieugrondslag, zoals deze luidt met ingang van 1 januari 2008
ShortDescription: This Regulation operationalizes the Environmental Taxes law by specifying procedures for measurement, tax exemption, and compliance in areas like water abstraction, energy use, and waste. It includes conditions for exemption, refund eligibility, technical recording, and administrative inspection. Enforcement responsibility lies with the tax authorities. It ensures structured environmental tax compliance and standardization.
SpecificTopic: Carbon Pricing

OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 9 oktober 1997, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Besluit luchtkwaliteit benzeen (Stb. 1993, 35), zoals dit laatstelijk is gewijzigd bij besluit van 22 september 1997, Stb. 456
ShortDescription: This decision orders the publication of the consolidated text of the Benzene Air Quality decree, originally from 1993 and last amended in 1997. The decree sets limit and target values for benzene concentration in ambient air, applying to major roads, certain industrial establishments, and gas stations. It mandates monitoring, inventorying, and reporting duties for provincial and municipal authorities to higher government bodies.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Benzene Air Quality Decree 1993; Decree of 22 Sept 1997
SpecificTopic: Air Quality; Ambient Air Quality Standards; Chemical Regulation
ApplicableSectors: Manufacturing; Energy Production and Utilities; Agriculture, Farming, and Fishing; Chemicals

OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 9 oktober 1997, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Besluit luchtkwaliteit stikstofdioxide (Stb. 1987, 33), zoals dit laatstelijk is gewijzigd bij besluit van 22 september 1997, Stb. 456
ShortDescription: This decision establishes limit values and guideline values for nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) concentrations in the ambient air of the Netherlands. It mandates measurement methods and monitoring requirements, assigning responsibilities to provincial and municipal executive councils for assessing pollution near roads and industrial areas. Authorities must report findings to higher levels, and the Minister periodically reviews the standards.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental Management Law; Air Pollution Law
SpecificTopic: Air Quality; Ambient Air Quality Standards; Industrial Emissions

ShortTitle: Air Quality (Carbon Monoxide and Lead) Decree
OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 9 oktober 1997, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Besluit luchtkwaliteit koolstofmonoxide en lood (Stb. 1987, 34), zoals dit laatstelijk is gewijzigd bij besluit van 22 september 1997, Stb. 456
ShortDescription: This decree establishes limit values for carbon monoxide and lead concentrations in the ambient air of the Netherlands. It defines measurement methods and monitoring requirements, detailing procedures for air sampling, analysis, and data processing. The regulation assigns responsibilities to provincial and municipal executive councils for inventorying and assessing air pollution, particularly from industrial facilities and motor vehicles. Authorities are required to report findings to higher levels of government, and the Minister is mandated to periodically review the established values.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental Management Law; Air Pollution Law
SpecificTopic: Air Quality; Ambient Air Quality Standards; Industrial Emissions
ApplicableSectors: Manufacturing; Energy Production and Utilities; Motor Vehicles

OriginalTitle: Beschikking van de Minister van Justitie van 9 oktober 1997, houdende plaatsing in het Staatsblad van de tekst van het Besluit luchtkwaliteit zwaveldioxide en zwevende deeltjes (zwarte rook) (Stb. 1986, 78), zoals dit laatstelijk is gewijzigd bij besluit van 22 september 1997, Stb. 456
ShortDescription: This decree defines national limit and guideline values for sulfur dioxide (SO₂) and black smoke (particulate matter) concentrations in ambient air. It mandates monitoring methods and charges provincial governments with measurement and reporting obligations. The regulation encourages inter-provincial consultation in cases of transboundary pollution. Although it does not impose sanctions itself, enforcement relies on broader legal frameworks.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Wet milieubeheer; Wet inzake de luchtverontreiniging
SpecificTopic: Air Quality; Ambient Air Quality Standards; Industrial Emissions
ApplicableSectors: Manufacturing; Energy Production and Utilities; Chemicals

OriginalTitle: Wet op de Economische Delicten
ShortDescription: This law defines "economic offenses" as violations of provisions under various laws including environmental, labor, and financial regulations. It classifies them by severity and prescribes penalties such as imprisonment, fines, business closure, and confiscation. It also outlines enforcement, prosecution, and appeal processes, and provides authorities with investigative powers.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental Management Law; Environmental and Planning Law
SpecificTopic: Compliance with Laws and Regulations; Industrial Emissions; Occupational Health and Safety

OriginalTitle: Wet op de omgeving en de ruimtelijke ordening
ShortDescription: This law integrates numerous environmental and spatial laws into a single framework governing air, water, soil, biodiversity, and infrastructure. It standardizes permitting, compliance, and enforcement procedures and emphasizes sustainable development and public participation.
SpecificTopic: Compliance with Laws and Regulations; Land Use Planning; Biodiversity Conservation
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E1: Climate Change; ESRS E2: Pollution; ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources; ESRS E4: Biodiversity and Ecosystems; ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy; ESRS S3: Affected Communities

OriginalTitle: Wet milieubeheer
ShortDescription: Historically foundational, this law regulated emissions, waste, and environmental permits. Though largely repealed by the Environmental and Planning law, it still implements EU directives, especially on emissions trading, and retains rules on waste management and hazardous substances.
Amendedordevelopedbyifany: Environmental and Planning Law; EU ETS Directive

ApplicableSectors: All Industries (Sector-Agnostic); Energy Production and Utilities

OriginalTitle: Wet op het financieel toezicht
ShortDescription: This law establishes a comprehensive framework for financial supervision in the Netherlands, covering both prudential and conduct aspects. It regulates institutions including banks, insurers, investment firms, and payment service providers, outlining licensing, governance, and market behavior standards. The law references EU directives and incorporates emission rights and green bonds into its scope, enabling supervision of sustainable finance. Supervisory authorities may impose administrative fines up to €40 million and enforce compliance directives.
SpecificTopic: Emissions Trading Schemes
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS 1: General Requirements; ESRS 2: General Disclosures
ApplicableSectors: Financial Institutions; Energy Production and Utilities

OriginalTitle: Klimaatakkoord
ShortDescription: This Agreement aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the Netherlands by 49% by 2030 compared to 1990 levels, in line with the Paris Climate Agreement. It was developed in cooperation with over 100 stakeholders across five sectors: Electricity, Industry, Mobility, Built Environment, and Agriculture & Land Use. It outlines binding and voluntary measures, including CO₂ pricing, regional energy strategies, innovation programs, and public participation requirements. Implementation is monitored under the Klimaatwet via annual reports, and enforcement is possible through legal, fiscal, and administrative mechanisms.
SpecificTopic: Climate Change Mitigation; Renewable Energy Targets; Emissions Trading Schemes
ClosestESRSTopics: ESRS E1: Climate Change; ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy; ESRS S3: Affected Communities
ApplicableSectors: All Industries (Sector-Agnostic); Energy Production and Utilities; Agriculture, Farming, and Fishing; Public Sector
